seo.title: Langfuse vs LangSmith vs Helicone
seo.description: Compare LLM observability platforms by tracing model, integration path, self-hosting, evaluations, gateway features, and operational fit.

content.intro: LLM failures hide inside prompts, retrieval steps, tool calls, provider retries, and model outputs, so ordinary HTTP metrics are rarely enough. Langfuse, LangSmith, and Helicone all expose token use, latency, and traces, but differ in data model, integration style, deployment control, and how closely observability is coupled to an orchestration ecosystem.

options.name: Langfuse
options.tagline: Open-source LLM engineering and observability platform
options.best_for: Teams wanting OpenTelemetry-friendly tracing, evaluations, prompt management, and a credible self-hosting path.

options.name: LangSmith
options.tagline: Managed tracing and evaluation platform from LangChain
options.best_for: Teams building heavily with LangChain or LangGraph that want tightly integrated debugging and evaluation workflows.

options.name: Helicone
options.tagline: Open-source AI gateway and observability platform
options.best_for: Teams that want fast proxy-based logging plus gateway controls across model providers.

values.Langfuse: SDKs, framework integrations, and OpenTelemetry ingestion
values.LangSmith: LangChain-native tracing plus SDK and OpenTelemetry paths
values.Helicone: Gateway or provider-compatible proxy, SDKs, and integrations

criteria.name: Trace structure
criteria.description: How multi-step application work is represented.

values.Langfuse: Sessions contain traces; traces contain nested observations
values.LangSmith: Runs form nested traces with projects, threads, and metadata
values.Helicone: Requests can be grouped into sessions and traces with custom properties

criteria.name: Fastest initial setup
criteria.description: The shortest route to useful request visibility.

values.Langfuse: Quick with an SDK or OpenTelemetry, but requires instrumentation choices
values.LangSmith: Very quick for LangChain or LangGraph applications
values.Helicone: Proxy base-URL changes can capture provider traffic with minimal code

criteria.name: Framework neutrality
criteria.description: How naturally the platform works across custom stacks.

values.Langfuse: Strong through OpenTelemetry and provider/framework integrations
values.LangSmith: Supports custom code but is most natural in the LangChain ecosystem
values.Helicone: Gateway approach is largely independent of orchestration framework

criteria.name: Self-hosting
criteria.description: Ability to operate the platform on infrastructure you control.

values.LangSmith: Primarily managed; self-hosted options are aimed at enterprise arrangements
values.Helicone: Open-source deployment is available alongside the managed service

criteria.name: Evaluation workflow
criteria.description: Support for datasets, scores, experiments, and quality checks.

values.Langfuse: Scores, datasets, experiments, and evaluation integrations
values.LangSmith: Deep dataset, evaluator, experiment, and online evaluation workflow
values.Helicone: Evaluation and feedback features complement request analytics

criteria.name: Gateway controls
criteria.description: Runtime routing, caching, retries, and provider management.

values.Langfuse: Observability-first; gateway behavior usually lives elsewhere
values.LangSmith: Observability and evaluation first rather than an inference gateway
values.Helicone: Gateway-native routing, caching, rate controls, and provider access

criteria.name: Data portability
criteria.description: How easily telemetry can participate in a broader observability stack.

values.LangSmith: OpenTelemetry support helps connect existing instrumentation
values.Helicone: APIs and open-source components support export, with proxy data centered in Helicone

criteria.name: Operational caution
criteria.description: The main issue to resolve before production rollout.

values.Langfuse: Plan storage, retention, and upgrades if self-hosting
values.LangSmith: Define data handling and avoid accidental capture of sensitive prompts
values.Helicone: Treat proxy routing as critical-path infrastructure and design bypass/failure behavior

verdict.summary: Langfuse offers the strongest open, telemetry-oriented middle ground; LangSmith is compelling for evaluation-heavy LangChain teams; Helicone is the shortest path when a gateway should provide both visibility and runtime controls. Whichever platform you choose, redact secrets and personal data before export and verify trace flushing in serverless runtimes.

use_cases.reason: Its open-source deployment and OTel-oriented data path fit teams that need infrastructure and telemetry control.
Its native integration and experiment workflow reduce setup for teams already using the LangChain stack.

use_cases.scenario: Multi-provider gateway with immediate request analytics
use_cases.reason: Proxy-based capture combines a low-friction integration with gateway controls.
